theme.title: Mixed / Stock-Picker's Market
theme.summary: Communication Svcs (+1.70%) and Consumer Discretionary (+0.89%) led the market today, while Utilities (-1.31%) and Technology (-0.99%) brought up the rear. Growth sectors outpaced the rest, averaging +0.53% versus -0.61% for defensives and -0.19% for value, consistent with risk appetite favoring higher-beta names. Major indices moved in a tighter band — S&P 500 +0.38%, Dow +0.29%, Nasdaq +0.62% — without one clearly dominating. Small caps moved roughly in line with the broader market, with the Russell 2000 at +0.39%. The VIX fell 5.03% to 15.67, which reads as an orderly rotation rather than panic-driven selling. Gold added 0.66% to $4,064.50, crude oil was little changed at $80.04, and bitcoin slipped 0.23% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
